# brainjar

> Shape how your AI thinks — composable identity, soul, persona, and rules for AI agents.

brainjar manages AI agent behavior through composable layers. Instead of one monolithic config file, you separate what the agent sounds like (soul), how it works (persona), and what constraints it follows (rules). Each layer is a markdown file.

## Install

```bash
bun install -g @brainjar/cli
# or
npm install -g @brainjar/cli
```

## Initialize

```bash
brainjar init --default
brainjar status
```

## Core Concepts

### Soul — who the agent is

Defines personality: tone, character, standards. The constant across all tasks. You probably have 1-2.

```bash
brainjar soul create mysoul --description "Direct and rigorous"
brainjar soul use mysoul
brainjar soul list
brainjar soul show [name] [--short]
brainjar soul drop <name>
```

### Persona — how the agent works

Defines role and workflow. Switch per task. Personas bundle rules via frontmatter:

```yaml
---
rules:
  - default
  - security
---
```

```bash
brainjar persona create <name> --description <text> [--rules default,security]
brainjar persona use <name> [--local]
brainjar persona list
brainjar persona show [name] [--short]
brainjar persona drop <name>
```

### Rules — what the agent must follow

Behavioral constraints. Single files or multi-file packs (directories).

```bash
brainjar rules create <name> --description <text> [--pack]
brainjar rules add <name> [--local]
brainjar rules remove <name>
brainjar rules list
brainjar rules show <name>
```

### Brain — a saved configuration

Named snapshot of soul + persona + rules. Activate in one command.

```bash
brainjar brain save <name>
brainjar brain use <name>
brainjar brain list
brainjar brain show <name>
brainjar brain drop <name>
```

## State Cascade

State merges: global → local → env. Each tier overrides the previous.

- Global: ~/.brainjar/state.yaml (all projects)
- Local: .brainjar/state.yaml (this project)
- Env: BRAINJAR_* variables (this session)

Environment variables:
- BRAINJAR_HOME — override ~/.brainjar/ location
- BRAINJAR_SOUL — override active soul
- BRAINJAR_PERSONA — override active persona
- BRAINJAR_IDENTITY — override active identity
- BRAINJAR_RULES_ADD — comma-separated rules to add
- BRAINJAR_RULES_REMOVE — comma-separated rules to remove

## Compose (Subagent Dispatch)

Assemble a full prompt from a brain or ad-hoc layers:

```bash
brainjar compose <brain> --task "Review the changes in src/sync.ts"
brainjar compose --persona reviewer --task "Review the changes"
```

## Templates

Every create command generates a template with structured sections. The agent reads the template and fills it in:

- Soul template: title + space for voice, character, standards
- Persona template: YAML frontmatter for rules + sections for direct mode, subagent mode, always
- Rule template: title + description + constraints section

## Packs

Export/import shareable brain bundles:

```bash
brainjar pack export <brain> [--out <dir>] [--name <name>] [--version <ver>] [--author <name>]
brainjar pack import <dir> [--force] [--merge] [--activate]
```

## Hooks

Auto-sync via Claude Code hooks:

```bash
brainjar hooks install [--local]
brainjar hooks status
brainjar hooks remove
```

## Other Commands

```bash
brainjar status [--sync] [--global|--local] [--short]
brainjar sync [--quiet]
brainjar shell [--brain|--soul|--persona|--identity|--rules-add|--rules-remove]
brainjar reset [--backend claude|codex]
brainjar identity create|list|show|use|drop|unlock|get|status|lock
```

## Backends

Supported: claude (default), codex

```bash
brainjar init --backend codex
brainjar reset --backend codex
```

## File Structure

```
~/.brainjar/
  souls/            # Voice and character
  personas/         # Role and workflow
  rules/            # Constraints (files or directories)
  brains/           # Saved configurations
  state.yaml        # Active selections
```

## Links

- Docs: https://brainjar.sh
- GitHub: https://github.com/brainjar-sh/brainjar-cli
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@brainjar/cli
